title: Longest Palindromic Subsequence
hints: Consider how palindromes are built from the outside in. If the first and last characters match, does that simplify the problem for the inner substring?
This is a classic interval dynamic programming problem. Define a state representing the longest palindromic subsequence within a specific substring range.
The base cases are empty substrings (length 0) and single characters (length 1). The answer for a range depends on whether its endpoints match or not.
